fields.name: Ibuprofen
fields.adult_dosing: 200 to 400 mg by mouth every 4 to 6 hours as needed, not exceeding 1200 mg in 24 hours without medical advice.
fields.pediatric_dosing: Weight-based, typically 5 to 10 mg per kg every 6 to 8 hours; use the dosing device that comes with pediatric formulations.
fields.how_to_take: Take with food or milk to reduce stomach upset. Swallow tablets whole with water.
fields.food_alcohol_interactions: Avoid regular alcohol use, which raises the risk of stomach bleeding.
fields.serious_side_effects: Stomach bleeding, kidney problems, allergic reaction, and increased cardiovascular risk with long-term high-dose use.
fields.missed_dose_guidance: Since it's taken as needed, take a missed dose when you remember unless it's close to the next one; don't double up.

fields.name: Acetaminophen
fields.adult_dosing: 500 to 1000 mg every 4 to 6 hours as needed, not exceeding 3000 to 4000 mg in 24 hours depending on the product label.
fields.pediatric_dosing: Weight-based, typically 10 to 15 mg per kg every 4 to 6 hours; always use the pediatric dosing chart or device provided.
fields.how_to_take: Can be taken with or without food. Swallow tablets whole with water.
fields.food_alcohol_interactions: Avoid regular or heavy alcohol use, which raises the risk of liver damage.
fields.common_side_effects: Generally well tolerated; mild nausea in some people.
fields.serious_side_effects: Liver damage with overdose or in combination with other acetaminophen-containing products.
fields.missed_dose_guidance: Taken as needed; take when remembered unless close to the next dose.
interaction_warnings: Other products containing acetaminophen (cold and flu combinations)

fields.name: Amoxicillin
fields.adult_dosing: 250 to 500 mg every 8 hours, or as prescribed, for the full course even if you feel better early.
fields.pediatric_dosing: Weight-based, prescribed by a clinician; typically given as a liquid suspension for young children.
fields.how_to_take: Can be taken with or without food; taking with food may reduce stomach upset.
fields.food_alcohol_interactions: No significant interaction with alcohol, though feeling unwell is best avoided while on antibiotics.
fields.serious_side_effects: Allergic reaction (hives, swelling, difficulty breathing), severe diarrhea from C. difficile infection.
fields.missed_dose_guidance: Take as soon as you remember; if it's almost time for the next dose, skip the missed one and continue the schedule. Complete the full course.
interaction_warnings: Hormonal contraceptives may be less effective

fields.name: Lisinopril
fields.adult_dosing: Typically 10 to 40 mg once daily, started low and adjusted by a clinician.
fields.pediatric_dosing: Used in some children with a clinician's weight-based dosing; not for general self-use.
fields.how_to_take: Take at the same time each day, with or without food.
fields.food_alcohol_interactions: Alcohol can increase the blood-pressure-lowering effect and cause dizziness.
fields.serious_side_effects: Swelling of the face or throat (angioedema), high potassium levels, kidney function changes.
fields.missed_dose_guidance: Take as soon as remembered; if it's nearly time for the next dose, skip the missed one. Don't double up.

fields.name: Atorvastatin
fields.adult_dosing: Typically 10 to 80 mg once daily, dose set by a clinician based on cholesterol targets.
fields.pediatric_dosing: Occasionally used in adolescents with familial high cholesterol under specialist care.
fields.how_to_take: Can be taken any time of day, with or without food, but stick to the same time daily.
fields.food_alcohol_interactions: Avoid grapefruit and grapefruit juice, which can raise drug levels; limit alcohol due to liver strain.
fields.serious_side_effects: Severe muscle breakdown (rare), liver enzyme elevation.
fields.missed_dose_guidance: Take as soon as remembered unless it's almost time for the next dose; don't double up.

fields.name: Metformin
fields.adult_dosing: Typically 500 to 1000 mg twice daily with meals, titrated by a clinician up to a maximum daily dose.
fields.pediatric_dosing: Used in some adolescents with type 2 diabetes under specialist guidance.
fields.how_to_take: Take with meals to reduce stomach upset.
fields.food_alcohol_interactions: Limit alcohol, which increases the risk of a rare but serious condition called lactic acidosis.
fields.common_side_effects: Diarrhea, nausea, metallic taste, especially when starting.
fields.serious_side_effects: Lactic acidosis (rare, more likely with kidney impairment), vitamin B12 deficiency with long-term use.
fields.missed_dose_guidance: Skip the missed dose if it's almost time for the next one; don't double up.
interaction_warnings: Contrast dye used in some imaging scans

fields.name: Omeprazole
fields.adult_dosing: Typically 20 to 40 mg once daily before breakfast for 4 to 8 weeks, or longer term as directed.
fields.pediatric_dosing: Weight-based dosing used under a clinician's guidance for reflux.
fields.how_to_take: Take before a meal, usually breakfast, swallowing the capsule whole.
fields.food_alcohol_interactions: No major interaction, though alcohol can worsen the reflux symptoms it treats.
fields.common_side_effects: Headache, stomach pain, mild diarrhea or constipation.
fields.serious_side_effects: Increased fracture risk and low magnesium with long-term use, higher risk of certain gut infections.
fields.missed_dose_guidance: Take as soon as remembered; if close to the next dose, skip the missed one.

fields.name: Sertraline
fields.adult_dosing: Typically started at 25 to 50 mg once daily, adjusted by a clinician up to 200 mg as needed.
fields.pediatric_dosing: Used in some adolescents for OCD under specialist supervision.
fields.how_to_take: Take at the same time each day, with or without food; morning or evening depending on how it affects your sleep.
fields.food_alcohol_interactions: Avoid alcohol, which can worsen mood symptoms and increase sedation.
fields.common_side_effects: Nausea, sleep changes, sexual side effects, dry mouth.
fields.serious_side_effects: Increased suicidal thoughts in young people early in treatment, serotonin syndrome when combined with other serotonergic drugs.
fields.missed_dose_guidance: Take as soon as remembered unless nearly time for the next dose; never double up.
interaction_warnings: Other serotonergic medications or supplements like St. John's Wort

fields.name: Levothyroxine
fields.adult_dosing: Dose is individualized based on blood tests, typically once daily.
fields.pediatric_dosing: Weight-based, individualized by a pediatric specialist.
fields.how_to_take: Take on an empty stomach, 30 to 60 minutes before breakfast, with water only.
fields.food_alcohol_interactions: Calcium, iron supplements, and coffee can reduce absorption if taken too close together.
fields.common_side_effects: Usually minimal when dosed correctly; too high a dose can cause palpitations, anxiety, or weight loss.
fields.serious_side_effects: Heart rhythm problems if the dose is too high, especially in older adults.
fields.missed_dose_guidance: Take as soon as remembered that day; don't double up the next day without checking with a clinician.

fields.name: Amlodipine
fields.pediatric_dosing: Used in some children with hypertension under specialist weight-based dosing.
fields.how_to_take: Take at the same time each day, with or without food.
fields.food_alcohol_interactions: Avoid grapefruit juice; alcohol can add to dizziness and blood-pressure-lowering effects.
fields.serious_side_effects: Significant low blood pressure, rarely worsened chest pain in unstable heart disease.
fields.missed_dose_guidance: Take as soon as remembered unless it's nearly time for the next dose.

fields.name: Metoprolol
fields.adult_dosing: Typically 25 to 100 mg once or twice daily depending on the formulation and condition treated.
fields.pediatric_dosing: Rarely used in children, only under specialist cardiology care.
fields.how_to_take: Take with or immediately after food to improve absorption; don't stop suddenly without medical advice.
fields.food_alcohol_interactions: Alcohol can add to dizziness and blood-pressure-lowering effects.
fields.common_side_effects: Fatigue, cold hands and feet, dizziness.
fields.serious_side_effects: Very slow heart rate, worsening heart failure symptoms, masking of low blood sugar signs in diabetes.
fields.missed_dose_guidance: Take as soon as remembered unless nearly time for the next dose; never stop abruptly.

fields.name: Albuterol
fields.adult_dosing: 1 to 2 puffs (90 to 180 mcg) every 4 to 6 hours as needed for symptoms.
fields.pediatric_dosing: Similar dosing with a spacer device, as advised by a pediatric clinician.
fields.how_to_take: Shake well, use with a spacer if available, and rinse the mouth after use.
fields.food_alcohol_interactions: No significant food or alcohol interaction.
fields.serious_side_effects: Chest pain or significant palpitations, worsening breathing despite frequent use (seek urgent care).
fields.missed_dose_guidance: Used as needed for symptoms rather than on a fixed schedule; no missed-dose concern.

fields.name: Loratadine
fields.adult_dosing: 10 mg once daily as needed for allergy symptoms.
fields.pediatric_dosing: 5 mg once daily for children 2 to 5 years old, 10 mg for 6 years and up, per product labeling.
fields.how_to_take: Can be taken with or without food.
fields.food_alcohol_interactions: Minimal interaction; less sedating than older antihistamines but caution combining with alcohol is still reasonable.
fields.serious_side_effects: Allergic reaction to the medication itself (uncommon).
fields.missed_dose_guidance: Taken as needed; take when remembered.
interaction_warnings: Few significant interactions at standard doses

fields.name: Cetirizine
fields.adult_dosing: 10 mg once daily as needed for allergy symptoms.
fields.pediatric_dosing: 2.5 to 5 mg once daily for younger children, 10 mg for 6 years and up, per product labeling.
fields.how_to_take: Can be taken with or without food, any time of day.
fields.food_alcohol_interactions: Can add to drowsiness with alcohol in some people.
fields.serious_side_effects: Allergic reaction to the medication itself (uncommon).
fields.missed_dose_guidance: Taken as needed; take when remembered.

fields.name: Aspirin
fields.adult_dosing: 325 to 650 mg every 4 to 6 hours for pain, or 75 to 100 mg once daily when used for heart protection as directed by a clinician.
fields.pediatric_dosing: Not recommended for children or teenagers with viral illness due to the risk of Reye's syndrome.
fields.how_to_take: Take with food or milk to reduce stomach upset.
fields.food_alcohol_interactions: Avoid regular alcohol use, which raises the risk of stomach bleeding.
fields.serious_side_effects: Stomach bleeding, allergic reaction, ringing in the ears at high doses.
fields.missed_dose_guidance: For daily low-dose use, take as soon as remembered unless it's nearly time for the next dose; don't double up.
